notes: Inbound PII redaction is now wired. apps/router/poller.py:1344-1347 runs both message_text and the persisted raw_user_text excerpt through redact_user_message(text, tenant) BEFORE the proactive/datetime/chat markers are prepended (markers added at line 1379), so the third-party LLM never sees the raw name/email/phone and the persisted apology excerpt has no raw PII. redact_user_message (apps/pii/redactor.py:248) has the expected signature (allow_user_name default True, fail-open). Although C01's disposition claimed Telegram-only (LINE/iOS out of scope), the committed worktree ALSO wires the other two inbound channels: apps/router/line_webhook.py:1248-1249 and apps/router/chat_views.py:226 (iOS sends redacted_text to the LLM while persisting verbatim user_text for the ?since= feed). So at the user-behaviour level the privacy guarantee now holds on all three channels. Test PollerInboundRedactionTest asserts [EMAIL_ADDRESS_1] appears in both body and excerpt and markers survive — 6/6 C01 tests pass.

Same root gap as FA-0913 and now closed for the live Telegram path (the poller, not the webhook view — poller deletes the webhook on startup, so redact_telegram_update has no production caller and redact_user_message is the correct seam, which is what was wired at poller.py:1346-1347). The stale enqueue docstring in pending_queue.enqueue_message_for_tenant was corrected to state callers redact inbound text before enqueuing and the queue never re-redacts the assembled body (pending_queue.py:357-368). LINE and iOS inbound seams are also wired in the committed worktree (line_webhook.py:1248, chat_views.py:226), so the asymmetry (inbound never minted placeholders) is resolved fleet-wide. Verified the redacted text is what flows into enqueue_message_for_tenant payload.

Disposition recorded action=noop (config.py deemed outside C01's file list), but the committed Phase-3 change actually DID edit apps/pii/config.py: DATE_OF_BIRTH is removed from TIER_POLICIES['starter']['entities'] and replaced with an explanatory comment (config.py:91-97). Verified at runtime TIER_POLICIES['starter']['entities'] no longer contains DATE_OF_BIRTH, and grep shows no remaining DATE_OF_BIRTH reference in apps/pii except the comment. The stale advertisement of unreachable coverage is gone — exactly the suggested fix. Harmless whitelist entry eliminated; no detector path affected.

Poller PENDING/PROVISIONING branch (poller.py:851-855) now derives lang = getattr(tenant.user, 'language', None) or 'en' and sends error_msg(lang, 'waking_up') instead of the hardcoded English literal, mirroring the webhook path. Verified error_msg('ja','waking_up') returns the Japanese translation distinct from English. A non-English subscriber on the live poller path now gets the localized waking-up message.

Poller SUSPENDED (not-trial, no-subscription) branch (poller.py:858-866) now sends error_msg(lang, 'suspended', billing_url=f'{frontend_url}/settings/billing') with lang from tenant.user.language, replacing the hardcoded English literal. Verified error_msg resolves localized 'suspended' for ja/fr and correctly substitutes the billing_url placeholder. The working billing link is preserved and copy is now localized for ~12 locales.

Poller callback dispatch now has a task_action: prefix case (poller.py:750-754) delegating to new _handle_task_action_callback (poller.py:1511-1534), which calls apps.router.task_action_callbacks.handle_task_action_callback(update, tenant), parses the returned JsonResponse, and runs it through _execute_telegram_response. The handler (task_action_callbacks.py:69-111) performs undo_task_action, edits the channel message to strike-through, and returns an answerCallbackQuery dict ({method:'answerCallbackQuery',...}) which _execute_telegram_response POSTs — so the tapped Undo button stops spinning AND the reconciliation change is reverted on the live poller path. Test PollerTaskActionCallbackDispatchTest passes (dispatched + answered).

_handle_update now tracks had_photo = bool(msg_data.get('photo')) (poller.py:942); when a photo was present but _upload_photo_to_workspace returned None (e.g. >5MB cap), it prepends '[Photo too large to process — please send a smaller image under 5 MB]' to message_text (poller.py:951-959) before forwarding, mirroring the document/voice 'couldn't process' markers. The agent is now told the image was not seen instead of replying blind. The marker is plain text and survives the subsequent inbound redaction. Minor residual (acknowledged in the original finding's optional suggestion): a non-size download failure also shows the 'too large' wording, but the core user-visible behaviour — agent no longer confidently answers an unseen photo — is fixed.

relay_ai_response_to_telegram (the production queue-drain Telegram relay) now parses [[button:Label|callback_data]] markers, strips them from the text, and builds an inline_keyboard with callback_data 'agent:<data>' (pending_queue.py:1640-1657). _send_telegram_markdown was extended with an optional reply_markup arg attached to the LAST chunk only, including the HTML-rejection plain-text fallback (pending_queue.py:1715-1762). The poller's existing agent:-prefixed callback receiver handles taps, so no receiver change needed. The stale 'no inline button parsing yet' comment was removed. Test TelegramRelayButtonParsingTest asserts the markup produces {agent:confirm_yes, agent:confirm_no} and that no [[button: literal leaks — passes.

get_forwarding_timeout (services.py:259-285) now imports BYO_SLOW_MODELS with the same defensive ImportError fallback used in pending_queue._resolve_chat_timeout, and computes is_reasoning = model in REASONING_MODELS or model in BYO_SLOW_MODELS — exactly mirroring _resolve_chat_timeout (pending_queue.py:329). A BYO Claude (Sonnet/Opus) tenant now gets REASONING_MODEL_TIMEOUT (240s) and is_reasoning=True consistently across both helpers, closing the maintainability/consistency gap. Tests ForwardingTimeoutByoSlowModelTest (slow + standard) pass.

RuntimeLessonSearchView.get now wraps the search QuerySet in list(...) inside the existing try/except, so a DB-execution failure during iteration (OperationalError/pgvector unavailable/network drop) is caught and mapped to the documented {error: search_failed} 500 envelope instead of raising an unguarded generic 500. The except clauses (ValueError + Exception) are intact. The materialize-inside-guard pattern is exactly the suggested fix; behaviour now matches the error-envelope contract on the DB-failure path.

RuntimeJournalSearchView.get replaced the bare min(int(limit),50) with _parse_positive_int(...) wrapped in try/except ValueError returning HTTP 400 {error: invalid_request}. Verified _parse_positive_int raises ValueError('must be an integer') on non-numeric input, so ?limit=abc now yields 400 not 500. The second location the original detail cited (the reconcile endpoint ~line 2724) already wraps its int cast in try/except (TypeError, ValueError) with a default fallback, so it was never a 500 bug; leaving it unchanged is correct. Sibling-endpoint consistency restored.

Added DAILY_SLUG_RE (^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$) and folded the daily-date check into validate_kind_slug() so kind=='daily' with a non-ISO slug returns ('invalid_slug', ...). All three write paths call validate_kind_slug and return its error: GET (1889), PUT (1944), and set-section (2301). The set-section path still defaults an empty daily slug to today's ISO date before validating. So a PUT/set-section with kind=daily slug=my-notes now 400s, closing the journal-context-bundle corruption (slug__gte text-compare) gap. Dedicated tests (test_daily_requires_iso_date_slug / accepts / non_daily_unaffected) pass.

RuntimeProfileUpdateView.patch now returns explicit 400s (invalid_display_name/invalid_language/invalid_location_city) for present-but-empty-after-strip or over-length values (>100 / >10 / >255), symmetric with the existing timezone (invalid_timezone) and coordinate (invalid_coordinates) paths. A present-but-invalid field is no longer silently dropped and misreported as no_changes, so the agent gets an actionable, distinct error. Matches the suggested fix.

search_lessons() now chains .prefetch_related('journal_entries','tutoring_sessions') BEFORE the [:limit] slice, mirroring recent_active_stars(). This eliminates the 2-extra-FK-queries-per-star N+1 when build_star_context(full=True) reads star.journal_entries.all()/tutoring_sessions.all() in the constellation q-mode path. Both related_names exist on Lesson (used elsewhere in the codebase). Prefetch is placed correctly before the slice so it actually applies. Lessons suite (106 tests) green.

create_connections() now reconciles similarity edges instead of strictly accreting: inside transaction.atomic() it deletes connection_type='similar' edges (both directions) to peers no longer in the current similar set, recreates current edges via get_or_create, and .update(similarity=...) refreshes weights on surviving similar edges. Operator precedence in the delete Q (& binds tighter than |) parses correctly as (from=lesson AND NOT to in peers) OR (to=lesson AND NOT from in peers). User-curated edge types (user_linked/builds_on/contradicts) are preserved because the filter is scoped to connection_type='similar' — verified by test_rewrite_preserves_user_curated_edges. test_rewrite_drops_stale_similar_edges proves both-direction stale-edge removal; the targeted (not delete-all) approach keeps the idempotency count at 0 when the set is unchanged. Full lessons suite (106) passes including the pre-existing idempotency assertion.

_handle_lesson_postback no longer returns silently for a non-pending lesson: it re-queries the lesson without the status filter and, if it exists in a resolved state, sends '👍 That one's already saved/already skipped!' via _send_line_follow_up (status=='approved' → saved, else skipped), mirroring the sibling _handle_extraction_postback ack. Silence remains only when the lesson truly does not exist. Lesson statuses ('approved'/'dismissed') match the branch logic; _send_line_follow_up exists and pushes a text message. Stale-button-feels-dead UX gap closed. Router LINE suite (255) green.

LINE side fully and correctly fixed: the generic-postback fallback in _handle_postback now runs a SUSPENDED check + check_budget pre-flight (with _hibernate_for_quota on over-budget) before _forward_to_container, mirroring _handle_message's gate, so a tapped generic button on an over-budget-but-still-LIVE tenant no longer skips the budget gate. Helpers (check_budget, build_status_bubble, error_msg, _hibernate_for_quota) are imported/available. HOWEVER the defect and its suggested_fix are explicitly cross-channel and name the Telegram poller path (poller.py:831-836) as needing the same fix. I verified the Telegram poller's 'agent:' generic button-tap branch (poller.py:826-843) still calls _forward_to_container and returns BEFORE the SUSPENDED/budget gates (poller.py:860-875); the C01 poller diff added gating only to the typed-message path, not the agent: branch. So the same exploit window (one billable turn via a tapped button on an over-budget-but-LIVE tenant) remains open on Telegram. Disposition flagged this as out-of-scope (C01 file), but the cross-channel user-story behaviour is not fully met → partial.

Primary onboarding case fixed: the LINE audio branch now resolves the tenant early and, if (not onboarding_complete or onboarding_step==0), sends the 'please type your answer' bubble and returns BEFORE _show_loading and the paid Whisper call; the resolution is reused downstream via prefetched_tenant/tenant_prefetched (no double resolve). The common fresh-onboarding voice case no longer wastes a Whisper transcript or shows a >10s loading animation. RESIDUAL: the defect explicitly framed the window as 'first interaction OR after re-introduction.' A re-introduction-eligible tenant has onboarding_complete=True and onboarding_step>0, so the EARLY gate (which runs before needs_reintroduction resets step to 0 at line ~1116) does NOT catch them — their voice message still incurs the full Whisper call before being rejected. needs_reintroduction only fires for backfilled legacy users (narrow population), but the named 'after re-introduction' sub-case is genuinely not covered → partial. Router LINE suite (255) green.

Scheduled-automations dispatcher is now FULLY wired (better than the disposition, which claimed only TASK_MAP was added and SYSTEM_CRONS was an external follow-up). TASK_MAP in apps/cron/views.py:203 maps 'run_due_automations' -> apps.automations.tasks.run_due_automations_task. AND register_system_crons.py:118 now has the QStash schedule ('run-due-automations', '* * * * *', '/api/cron/trigger/run_due_automations/') — added in the same fix commit. Traced the full path: SYSTEM_CRONS -> /api/cron/trigger/run_due_automations/ -> TASK_MAP -> run_due_automations_task (tasks.py:8) -> run_due_automations (scheduler.py:30), which filters status=ACTIVE + next_run_at<=now, orders by next_run_at, limits to DEFAULT_DUE_BATCH_SIZE=100, calls execute_automation(trigger_source=SCHEDULE), catches per-automation exceptions into 'errors', returns summary dict. Verified import resolution at runtime (import_string resolved run_due_automations_task). A subscriber's ACTIVE automation will now fire automatically every minute once register_system_crons runs post-deploy. Expected behaviour met.

The dedup_crons fleet fan-out branch no longer hardcodes 'failed': 0. apps/cron/views.py:1356-1357 now computes failed = len(batch_tasks) - enqueued and returns {enqueued, failed}, identical to the sibling broadcast endpoint at views.py:1296-1297. On publish_batch failure the except block forces enqueued=0 (views.py:1352-1354), so failed now equals the number of dropped tasks instead of masking a total drop as a no-op; partial sync-fallback counts (enqueued < len(tasks)) are also surfaced. Operator telemetry now distinguishes 'every dedup task dropped' from 'no active tenants matched'. Expected behaviour met.

Took the documented lowest-risk path (suggested-fix option: accept per-task idempotency as the real guard, remove the misleading TTL comment). The 'covers slowest fleet rollout' comment is gone; apps/cron/views.py:1689-1697 now accurately states the lock only guards the millisecond enqueue (eligibility query + publish_batch), is released in finally, and that double-bumps are prevented at the task layer (bump_openclaw_atomic_per_tenant re-checks version+image_tag at execution; bump_openclaw_version_for_tenant snapshots-before-write/restores-on-failure). TTL narrowed from 30min to 5min as an enqueue mutex with worker-death orphan headroom (views.py:1698). This was a comment/telemetry-accuracy defect (low severity, logic category); the lock lifecycle behaviour is intentionally unchanged and the documentation now matches reality. Expected behaviour (409 concurrency lock on the enqueue, per-task idempotency for the rollout) holds.

Sub-claim (a) count-mismatch is FULLY fixed (disposition is stale — it claimed this was reverted, but the code shows it was applied). apps/finance/snapshot.py:35-39 now guards 'created_count += 1' on a non-None return from _create_snapshot_for_tenant, so duplicate-skip and no-accounts-skip (both return None) no longer inflate the count; docstring 'number of snapshots created' is now truthful. The two existing tests were correctly updated to count==0 semantics (apps/finance/tests.py:1438 'duplicate skipped -> nothing newly created', :1449 'nothing created'), and SnapshotServiceTests (5 tests) PASS. Sub-claim (b) scheduling is NOT done: grep finds no create_monthly_snapshots task in apps/finance/tasks.py (only schedule_finance_welcome_task), no TASK_MAP entry in apps/cron/views.py, and no SYSTEM_CRONS schedule — so create_monthly_snapshots still never runs in production. Partial is the right verdict: the live-impacting docstring-vs-behaviour half is fixed and test-verified; the scheduling half is an acceptable documented dormant-feature follow-up (defect itself notes feature is latent with no production consumer).

The lockout/data-integrity gap is closed via the suggested-fix option (a): the previously-stubbed --resend-only flag is now implemented. apps/tenants/management/commands/rotate_all_passwords.py:74-83 declares the flag; handle() short-circuits to _handle_resend_only (line 94-95); _handle_resend_only (lines 170-224) targets users with an unusable password (has_usable_password() is False, owner-excluded), re-sends the reset email, and does NOT re-bump password_last_changed_at — so it recovers email-failed users regardless of the --since cutoff (the case that the documented same---since retry previously skipped forever). New test tests_audit_C03.py:test_resend_only_recovers_email_failed_user asserts the flaky-email user is recovered and bob's password_last_changed_at is unchanged (lines 64-84). The 3 new C03 tests PASS and all 33 existing privacy-rotation tests PASS (serial run; the earlier 3 'connection is closed' errors were a parallel-DB flake, not logic). Expected behaviour met.

Same root cause as FA-1071, addressed by the same --resend-only implementation, which provides an automated recovery path that does NOT depend on the operator remembering an earlier --since. The module docstring was updated (lines 15-19) to document --resend-only as the recovery mechanism instead of a 'future enhancement', and the email-failure ERROR line now instructs 're-run with --resend-only to re-send their reset link' (lines 162-168). The footgun (naive same---since retry silently skipping locked-out users) now has a first-class command-driven fix. Verified via new tests + full existing suite passing. Expected behaviour met.

Operator-summary undercount fixed exactly per suggested fix. The green headline is scoped to 'Rotated + emailed (got reset link): {rotated}' (line 153), and a new explicit line 'Total passwords rotated / locked out: {rotated + email_failed}' (lines 156-161, WARNING style) now surfaces the true blast radius as a single number, so a partial-Mailgun-outage run cannot be misread by reading only the green line. The distinct red ERROR line for email_failed is retained (lines 162-168). Expected behaviour (operator sees the real locked-out count) met.

Unreachable checkoutError banner removed from the no-tenant empty-state branch. The diff deletes the {checkoutError && (...)} block that lived in the else branch which has no checkout control. checkoutError state/setter remain genuinely used in the hasTenant branch (set in handleCheckout at lines 37/48/50, rendered at line 169), so no dead state was orphaned. Matches suggested_fix exactly; maintainability defect resolved, no behaviour regression.

useDonationPreferenceMutation.onMutate now recomputes donation_amount client-side inside the optimistic setQueryData, mirroring the server formula round(surplus*pct/100,4). effectiveEnabled/effectivePct derived from newData ?? old; donation_amount = (enabled && surplus>0) ? Math.round(surplus*pct/100*10000)/10000 : 0; merged alongside ...newData. This keeps the StatCard impact dollars, cost-split legend, split-bar widths and mealsEstimate consistent with the toggled percentage during the optimistic window instead of leaving them stale until onSettled refetch. The 4-decimal rounding is correctly implemented.

pollUntilDone timeout branch no longer stops watching at POLL_TIMEOUT_MS. It now sets longRunning (copy 'Still composing…/I'll message you') but re-schedules tick at SLOW_POLL_INTERVAL_MS (30s), capped at POLL_HARD_TIMEOUT_MS (~20min past the fast window). The re-scheduled tick re-runs the full status checks, so a post-timeout status=failed flips phase to 'failed' with the in-page error (surfacing retry) and a late status=ready/delivered still resolves the toast+library. This fully satisfies the frontend story in expected_behaviour. The disposition correctly scoped out the backend defense-in-depth channel-ping on _fail() (apps/core/services.py is outside cluster C04); that was an optional 'backend defense-in-depth' suggestion, not part of the frontend expected_behaviour, so the frontend stranding symptom is fully fixed — pass, not partial.

Added openArchiveDialog (calls archiveMutation.reset() then setConfirmArchive) and closeArchiveDialog (reset() then setConfirmArchive(null)). AccountCard onArchive for both debt and savings lists wired to openArchiveDialog; ConfirmArchiveDialog onCancel wired to closeArchiveDialog. Resetting the shared mutation on both open and close prevents the stale isError/error from a prior failed archive leaking into a freshly-mounted dialog, including the same-account re-open case the defect called out. Matches the preferred suggested_fix (reset() approach).

ArchivedAccountsSection is now rendered inside the zero-active-accounts empty-state branch (after the 'No accounts yet' card) with props identical to the existing main-block render (expanded/onToggle/isLoading/accounts/onRestore/restoringId). The component renders the aria-expanded toggle with ▾/▴ chevrons, Loading state, 'No archived accounts.' empty text, and per-account Restore button that fires unarchiveMutation.mutate(id) showing 'Restoring…'/disabled. A subscriber who archives every account can now reach the Restore path without first creating a new account. Wiring matches expected_behaviour and suggested_fix.

Added validationError state. handleSubmit now sets a specific message before each early return ('Add a template name.' / 'Add a slug for the template.' / 'Add at least one section with both a slug and a title.') instead of silently returning, and clears it on the valid path. Rendered in a rose panel (border-rose-border/bg-rose-bg/text-rose-text) above the createMutation/updateMutation error panels. Cleared on handleStartCreate, handleStartEdit, and on Name/Slug onChange edits. A user who forgets the Name now gets explicit guidance instead of a dead button. Matches the preferred local-error suggested_fix.
evidence: frontend/app/journal/templates/page.tsx:83 (state), 92,103 (clear on start), 128-140 (per-field messages), 196-198,206-213 (clear on edit), 308-312 (rose panel)

notes: Invalid Tailwind class pl-13 (silently dropped by JIT — no '13' spacing step in v3.4) replaced with arbitrary value pl-[3.25rem] (52px = 40px icon + 12px gap), restoring the intended mobile indent so Monthly/Balance figures align under the nickname; sm:pl-0 preserved for desktop. Grep confirms no remaining pl-13 reference and no '13' key was added to tailwind.config.ts (scoped component fix per disposition). Resolves the mobile-only misalignment.

WorkoutDuplicateView now dates the copy with today_in_tenant_tz(tenant) instead of bare date_cls.today() (UTC). The helper (apps/common/llm_contracts.py:121-123) derives the date from the tenant's own IANA timezone via dj_tz.now().astimezone(tenant_tz(tenant)).date() — actually more robust than the suggested timezone.localdate() because it does not depend on request-middleware tz activation. Regression test test_duplicate_uses_tenant_local_date freezes the clock just after JST midnight (UTC still prior day) and asserts the duplicate lands on 2026-06-02 (tenant-local), status=planned. An east-of-UTC tenant duplicating in the early-morning window now gets the correct calendar day. Test suite (7 tests) passes.

RestingHRDetailView.patch now has the missing date-conflict guard: after is_valid(), reads serializer.validated_data.get('date') and, if it differs from entry.date and another RestingHeartRateLog row exists for the same tenant/date, returns a clean 409 {error:date_conflict} before serializer.save(). This exactly mirrors the BodyWeightDetailView guard (views.py:626-632). Moving a resting-HR entry onto an occupied date now returns 409 instead of an opaque 500 IntegrityError. Test test_resting_hr_patch_date_collision_returns_409 (+ same-date-still-ok) passes.

SleepDetailView.patch now has the identical 409 date_conflict guard (filters SleepLog by tenant/new_date, excludes self) before serializer.save(), mirroring BodyWeight. PATCH moving a sleep entry onto an occupied date returns a clean 409 instead of a 500. Test test_sleep_patch_date_collision_returns_409 (+ move-to-free-date-ok) passes.

Both halves of the regen-boundary UTC drift are fixed. Consumer WorkoutPlanDetailView.patch (views.py) replaced the local 'from datetime import date as _date; today = _date.today()' with today = today_in_tenant_tz(tenant) at line 1247 (tenant in scope from line 1228); used for both the delete date__gte filter and regen_start. Runtime RuntimeWorkoutPlanDetailView.patch now passes today=today_in_tenant_tz(tenant) into reconcile_plan_state (runtime_views.py:1430-1432); services.reconcile_plan_state already exposes the today= keyword seam (services.py:96). services.py needed no edit (per disposition). East-of-UTC tenants editing a plan in early-morning local hours no longer get a 1-day-prior regen boundary.

RuntimeWorkoutPlanListCreateView.post now wraps WorkoutPlan.objects.create() AND _expand_plan_workouts() in a single transaction.atomic() inside the existing try/except (returns 400 create_failed on failure). A mid-loop expansion failure now rolls back the plan row too, so the (tenant,name,start_date,status=active) idempotency short-circuit above can no longer match a partial orphan and lock in a structurally-incomplete plan; the retry recreates cleanly. workouts_created is assigned inside the atomic block and correctly referenced afterward (line 1298) — bound on every success path since the except always returns. Matches the sibling swap view's atomic pattern. No dedicated regression test for the atomicity/dedup-lockin scenario exists (the test-file header claims behavioural coverage but I found none specific to this defect), however the code fix is present, correct, and complete; verdict pass on code-path inspection.

RuntimeWorkoutPlanDetailView.patch no longer stores schedule_json after a bare isinstance(dict) check. It now runs _validate_normalize_schedule(raw_schedule) (the same validate_detail/normalize_detail contract the POST path enforces, returning the LLMValidationError 400 envelope on bad strength/calisthenics sets) and also validates week_overrides via _validate_normalize_week_overrides for POST parity. The subtle reconciler hazard is correctly handled: _validate_normalize_schedule always injects a detail_json key (line 953), which the reconciler would treat as 'clear it'; the fix strips that injected key for days where the caller supplied no detail_json, preserving the 'silence = leave alone' contract. Tests prove a negative-rep set is rejected 400 and does NOT mutate plan.schedule_json, and a valid set is accepted 200 and persists. Malformed sets can no longer bypass validation into future Workout.detail_json via reconcile/apply.

Disposition reported action=partial/noop claiming the fix line (apps/fuel/runtime_views.py:1510, change bare date.today() to today_in_tenant_tz(tenant)) was outside cluster C06's allowed files. However the HEAD commit (HEAD~1..HEAD diff) DID apply this fix — RuntimeFuelAuditView.get() now computes `today = today_in_tenant_tz(tenant)` (runtime_views.py:1549) instead of `date.today()`. This tenant-local today correctly drives the daily-note slug lookup (slug=str(today), line 1554) and the 14-day planned-workout window (date__gte=today, line 1564), so east-of-UTC tenants no longer resolve 'today' as yesterday in the 00:00-09:00 local window. No bare date.today() remains in executable code (only in explanatory comments at lines 1241 and 1546). Whichever cluster owns runtime_views.py applied it; the user-facing morning-window advisory drift is resolved. Verified by inspecting the diff and the current code path.
evidence: git diff HEAD~1 runtime_views.py: -today = date.today() +today = today_in_tenant_tz(tenant)

notes: Fix present and correct: '_fuel:' added to _UNMANAGED_PREFIXES at apps/orchestrator/cron_reconcile.py:49 (now ('_sync:', '_fuel:')) with an accurate explanatory comment. _is_unmanaged_cron (cron_reconcile.py:80-95) matches names via name.startswith(_UNMANAGED_PREFIXES), so live _fuel:* gateway crons are now treated as unmanaged by the main diff, the dedup pre-pass, and the at-cron janitor — they never enter to_remove and are never cron.removed. This leaves regenerate_fuel_crons as the sole writer, matching the design. Regression test test_fuel_session_cron_survives_tenant_reconcile asserts a _fuel:{8-hex} kind=cron job with no CronJob row is NOT passed to cron.remove and summary['removed']==0. Test passes. The destructive flapping race against the fuel reconciler is closed.

Fix present and correct: a hibernation/suspension guard was pushed down into regenerate_fuel_crons itself (apps/orchestrator/fuel_cron.py:133-148). After the use_session_scheduling check and before any invoke_gateway_tool('cron.list') call, it returns the zero summary when tenant.hibernated_at is not None or tenant.status == SUSPENDED. Centralizing in regenerate_fuel_crons (not just reconcile_fuel_crons_task) means the hourly fleet sweep, the per-tenant task, and any other caller all inherit the guard, so they cannot drift again. This prevents the doomed cron.list POST to a scaled-to-zero container (GatewayError + full stack trace + inflated error telemetry every hour). Reconcile-on-wake still rebuilds the crons, so no data loss. Tests test_skips_hibernated_tenant_without_gateway_call and test_skips_suspended_tenant_without_gateway_call assert invoke_gateway_tool is never called and errors==0. Both pass.

Fix present and correct: apply_single_tenant_config_task (apps/orchestrator/tasks.py:193-202) now, after refresh_from_db(), re-checks the SAME guard update_tenant_config uses (status != ACTIVE or not container_id — verified identical to services.py:574) and returns WITHOUT advancing config_version when it holds. So if the tenant flips SUSPENDED / loses its container between the version gate and the write, config_version stays behind pending_config_version, letting apply_pending_configs / billing reactivation / idle-wake / manual force_apply (all gated on pending>config) naturally retry once the tenant is ACTIVE again. After execute_automation returns, the view inspects run.status and returns HTTP 502 BAD_GATEWAY with the serialized FAILED run (AutomationRunSerializer exposes status + error_message) instead of an unconditional 201; success still returns 201. This resolves the REST-semantics mismatch from the defect: a subscriber clicking 'Run now' against a non-ACTIVE tenant / missing container_fqdn / missing telegram_chat_id (which _dispatch_to_openclaw raises AutomationExecutionError -> swallowed at services.py:469-471 into a FAILED run) now gets a failure HTTP status, not 201. Manual keys include uuid4 so created=True always, meaning run.status reflects the real dispatch outcome and is never a stale short-circuit. AutomationRunSerializer is imported (views.py:17). The fixer chose 502 over the alternative 409; 502 is the more accurate upstream-dispatch-failure mapping. expected_behaviour (limits -> 400, success -> 201) is preserved; the only intentional deviation is FAILED -> 502, which is the intended improvement.

Self-healing fix present and correct. STALE_RUNNING_THRESHOLD=15min added (services.py:29). In execute_automation, when get_or_create returns an existing row (created=False), it now computes is_stale_running = status==RUNNING and started_at is not None and (now - started_at) >= 15min; only non-stale rows short-circuit (return run). A stale RUNNING row falls through to the re-dispatch block (lines 457-494): re-marks started_at, dispatches via _dispatch_to_openclaw, and for SCHEDULE triggers calls _advance_schedule (line 490-492) which advances next_run_at to scheduled_for+1s. Traced the original infinite loop: scheduler.py:48 passes scheduled_for=automation.next_run_at, key=_build_idempotency_key=schedule:<id>:<epoch(next_run_at)> (services.py:240-242); with next_run_at unadvanced the same key was rebuilt every tick and get_or_create returned the stranded RUNNING row forever. With the fix, after one re-dispatch next_run_at moves forward, the next tick builds a new key and creates a fresh PENDING run -> loop broken, automatic recovery without operator intervention. Terminal rows (succeeded/failed/skipped) and fresh RUNNING rows (genuine concurrent in-flight) still short-circuit unchanged, preserving concurrent-duplicate protection. timezone imported (services.py:13). The fixer chose the in-scope re-dispatch path over the suggested reaper cron (which would touch out-of-cluster files); functionally equivalent for recovery. Residual: a fresh hard-kill within the 15-min window after re-dispatch could re-strand, but it self-heals on the next tick past the threshold, so the permanent-wedge defect is genuinely eliminated.

notes: The fix is present and replaces the buggy first-match-by-state scan with a per-node AST line lookup: input component destructures `node` and uses `node?.position?.start.line`, converting 1-based to 0-based via `sourceLine - 1` (markdown-renderer.tsx:40,45). The wiring is correct (document-view.tsx:542 passes `content={doc.markdown}`; handleCheckboxToggle at :357-371 splits that same string by '\n', so indices would align). HOWEVER the chosen mechanism does not work in this library stack. With react-markdown ^10.1.0 + remark-gfm ^4.0.1 + remark-breaks ^4.0.0, the GFM task-list checkbox `input` element is a SYNTHETIC hast node created by mdast-util-to-hast's listItem handler (node_modules/mdast-util-to-hast/lib/handlers/list-item.js) WITHOUT any `position` assigned — only the enclosing `<li>` carries position. I confirmed this empirically by running the exact pipeline (remarkParse+remarkGfm+remarkBreaks+remarkRehype) over a 3-checkbox document: every `input` node reported `position: undefined` while the parent `li` reported correct line numbers (1,2,3). react-markdown passes this position-less node to the custom component (passNode=true, hast-util-to-jsx-runtime line 340). Consequence: `sourceLine` is always undefined, so `disabled={!onCheckboxToggle || sourceLine == null}` evaluates to true and `handleChange` early-returns on `sourceLine == null`. Net effect: ALL editable journal task-list checkboxes are now rendered disabled and inert. The original silent wrong-item mutation is technically gone (nothing mutates at all), but the expected_behaviour — clicking a checkbox toggles and persists the CORRECT item — is not met; the feature is fully broken instead of subtly wrong. The disposition's claim that `node.position.start.line` is available on the checkbox input is false for this version stack. The suggested_fix's second alternative (running ordinal counter over emitted checkboxes mapped to the nth task-list line) or reading position from the parent `li` would have worked; the AST-position approach as implemented does not.

notes: Fix present and correct for the documented interim guard, but the full expected behaviour (iOS-only users can receive AND act on a gate confirmation) is not met. VERIFIED PRESENT: send_gate_confirmation no longer reads tenant.user.preferred_channel directly; it now calls resolve_user_channel(tenant.user) (lazily imported from apps.router.cron_delivery) at messaging.py:327-329. resolve_user_channel (cron_delivery.py:48-83) honours preferred_channel when that channel is linked, falls back to any linked Telegram/LINE channel, returns 'app' for DeviceToken-only iOS users, and None when no surface exists. Since _SENDERS (messaging.py:305-308) maps only 'telegram'/'line', both 'app' and None hit the no-sender branch, which now emits a single clear warning naming action id, tenant, and resolved channel ('action will expire without user confirmation') at messaging.py:336-343 instead of the old misleading 'no Telegram chat_id' deep failure. CORRECTNESS: Telegram and LINE users route exactly as before (preferred honoured, msg_id stored in platform_message_id, channel in platform_channel) — no adjacent breakage. Confirmed by running apps.actions.tests_audit_C33 (4/4 pass) and the full apps.actions suite (44/44 pass) under the project venv; the warning fires for both resolved-channel 'app' and None as designed. WHY PARTIAL (not pass): the suggested_fix's full remediation — deliver the confirmation to iOS-only users as an APNs push + a ?since= gate feed row, plus an app-side approve/deny endpoint wrapping GateRespondView so the iOS app can act — is deliberately NOT implemented (it requires apps/router/* and apps/actions/views.py, outside cluster C33's single-file scope). This is a genuine improvement with a clearly documented external follow-up, matching the disposition's 'partial' action and the FA-0006 suggested_fix's explicit 'Minimum interim guard' — exactly the iOS APNs-gate-path follow-up case that warrants partial rather than pass.

notes: The fix is present and correct. The old entitled_active() used negative-exclude logic (.exclude(is_trial=True, trial_ends_at__lte=now, stripe_subscription_id='')) which only filtered tenants where ALL three conditions matched simultaneously. Ghost tenants (is_trial=False, no subscription, not budget-exempt) had is_trial=False so they did not match the exclude clause and passed through, receiving spurious cron seeding/config-apply cycles until the daily expire_trials sweep caught them. The fix replaces this with positive-inclusion logic (.filter(Q(stripe_subscription_id__gt='') | Q(is_trial=True, trial_ends_at__gt=now) | Q(is_budget_exempt=True))) which exactly mirrors the complement of _unentitled_active_tenants() in apps/cron/views.py, including the is_budget_exempt arm that protects canary/internal accounts. A ghost tenant now fails to match any of the three positive conditions and is correctly excluded. Eight regression tests in apps/tenants/tests_audit_C53.py cover: ghost excluded, paid included, unexpired trial included, expired trial excluded, budget-exempt included, suspended excluded, no-container excluded, and has_entitlement/entitled_active() agreement for ghosts.
